full_prompt: Propose a neuroscience study that builds on Neuropixels recordings and Kilosort spike sorting. Current pipelines recover many single units but still struggle to quantify stable identity of neurons across multi-day behavior sessions. Target a better longitudinal stability metric for chronic large-scale electrophysiology.

Propose a method inspired by Suite2p and CaImAn for calcium-imaging analysis. These tools perform well on relatively clean two-photon data but degrade when motion, neuropil contamination, and overlapping dendrites co-occur. Target cheaper compute while preserving cell-detection accuracy in dense cortical recordings.

Propose a research project extending Dynamic Causal Modeling and Granger causality for fMRI connectivity. Existing approaches often infer directed interactions from slow BOLD signals without adequately separating vascular delay from neural delay. Target a better metric for directionality in human resting-state networks.

Propose a study using CLARITY and iDISCO tissue-clearing approaches as starting points. Whole-brain clearing enables 3D anatomy but antibody penetration and imaging time remain bottlenecks for large mammalian brains. Target a new scalable domain: mesoscale mapping of primate subcortical circuits.

Propose a computational neuroscience project based on predictive coding and active inference. These frameworks explain perception and action but are often validated with abstract tasks rather than real neural population data. Target a better bridge between theory and Neuropixels-scale recordings during naturalistic behavior.

Propose a study that builds on optogenetic stimulation and closed-loop DBS. Current stimulation protocols can reduce symptoms but are often tuned empirically and ignore fast neural state changes. Target a better adaptive control metric for Parkinsonian beta oscillations.

Propose a project extending U-Net and Cellpose for histological neuron segmentation. Existing segmentation models generalize poorly across stains, species, and microscope settings. Target a new domain-general benchmark for automated neuron and glia counting.

Propose a research plan using fMRI representational similarity analysis and encoding models. These methods identify stimulus-related geometry but often miss temporal dynamics present in perception and memory. Target a better metric for time-resolved representational change using combined fMRI and MEG.

Propose a study inspired by BrainNetCNN and graph neural networks for connectome classification. Current connectome models can predict disease labels but are difficult to interpret and sensitive to site-specific scanner effects. Target better clinical robustness for multi-site psychiatric neuroimaging.

Propose a method building on DeepLabCut and SLEAP for animal pose estimation. These tools track behavior well in controlled videos but struggle with occlusion, multi-animal identity swaps, and low-light recordings. Target a cheaper and more reliable behavioral phenotyping pipeline for freely interacting rodents.

Propose a project using single-cell RNA-seq and Patch-seq as prior approaches. Transcriptomic cell types are increasingly detailed, but mapping them onto electrophysiological function remains incomplete in human cortical tissue. Target a better cross-modal taxonomy of inhibitory interneurons.

Propose a study extending spatial transcriptomics and MERFISH for brain mapping. These methods preserve molecular location but are costly and limited in throughput for large cohorts. Target cheaper compute and experimental design for comparing neurodegenerative disease progression across brain regions.

Propose a neuroscience project based on Hodgkin-Huxley models and generalized integrate-and-fire models. Biophysical models are interpretable but computationally expensive, while simplified models often miss dendritic nonlinearities. Target a cheaper surrogate model that preserves key spike-timing and dendritic computation metrics.

Propose a study building on ICA and artifact subspace reconstruction for EEG preprocessing. Existing methods remove eye and muscle artifacts but can also discard neural signals during movement-rich tasks. Target a better artifact-separation metric for mobile EEG in natural behavior.

Propose a project inspired by MNE-Python and FieldTrip for MEG source localization. Current inverse solutions depend strongly on head models and regularization choices, limiting reproducibility across labs. Target a better uncertainty estimate for cortical source reconstruction.

Propose a research plan using diffusion tensor imaging and constrained spherical deconvolution. DTI is limited in crossing-fiber regions, while more advanced tractography can produce false-positive connections. Target a better validation metric for human white-matter tract reconstruction.

Propose a study extending AlphaFold-style protein modeling and Rosetta for synaptic protein complexes. Structural predictions have improved for isolated proteins but remain weaker for dynamic membrane-bound synaptic assemblies. Target a new domain: activity-dependent conformational modeling of postsynaptic density proteins.

Propose a project using CRISPR screens and Perturb-seq in neural cells. Current perturbation studies often use simplified cell cultures that lack mature synaptic activity and circuit context. Target a better functional-genomic map of genes controlling human neuronal excitability.

Propose a study building on organoid protocols and assembloid fusion models. Brain organoids capture aspects of early development but lack consistent vascularization, sensory input, and mature circuit oscillations. Target a new domain for modeling neurodevelopmental disorders with measurable network activity.

Propose a method extending Gaussian process factor analysis and LFADS for neural population dynamics. Existing latent-variable models uncover low-dimensional structure but can overfit short trials and obscure single-neuron contributions. Target a better metric for interpretable dynamics during decision-making.

Propose a project inspired by hippocampal place-cell decoding and Bayesian position reconstruction. Current decoders work in simple arenas but degrade in complex, changing environments with remapping. Target a better metric for spatial-code stability during flexible navigation.

Propose a study using sharp-wave ripple detection and closed-loop optogenetic disruption. Prior work links ripples to memory consolidation, but most interventions use simple amplitude thresholds that miss event heterogeneity. Target a better event-classification metric for causal tests of episodic memory.

Propose a research project extending the forced oscillation paradigm and auditory steady-state response in schizophrenia. These EEG measures reveal abnormal gamma synchrony but have limited individual-level diagnostic power. Target a better biomarker metric for stratifying psychosis subtypes.

Propose a study based on transcranial magnetic stimulation and TMS-EEG. Current protocols can probe cortical excitability but are noisy, uncomfortable, and sensitive to coil placement. Target a cheaper and more reproducible metric of cortical inhibition in depression.

Propose a project using reinforcement learning models and drift-diffusion models for behavioral neuroscience. These models explain choices but often treat motivation, arousal, and movement vigor as nuisance variables. Target a better computational metric that links neuromodulatory state to decision dynamics.

Propose a study extending fiber photometry and genetically encoded dopamine sensors such as dLight. Fiber photometry captures bulk neuromodulator signals but cannot easily distinguish local release from movement and hemodynamic artifacts. Target a better metric for dopamine dynamics during natural social behavior.

Propose a project inspired by two-photon holographic stimulation and all-optical physiology. Existing all-optical systems can write and read neural activity but are expensive and limited in volume and speed. Target cheaper compute and optics for causal circuit mapping in mouse cortex.

Propose a study building on microglia morphology analysis and Sholl analysis. These approaches quantify branching but poorly capture dynamic immune states and cell-cell interactions in living brain tissue. Target a better metric for microglial surveillance in neuroinflammation.

Propose a research plan using PET amyloid imaging and tau PET imaging. These biomarkers detect Alzheimer’s pathology but are expensive and imperfect predictors of cognitive decline. Target a cheaper multimodal metric combining blood biomarkers with MRI for early disease staging.

Propose a method extending canonical correlation analysis and shared response modeling for naturalistic neuroimaging. Current approaches align subjects during movies but may ignore individual semantic interpretations and attention differences. Target a better metric for individual variability in naturalistic cognition.

Propose a study using Bayesian brain models and hierarchical predictive coding in autism research. Existing theories explain sensory hypersensitivity but are difficult to connect to measurable circuit mechanisms. Target a better experimental metric linking sensory prediction errors to cortical excitation-inhibition balance.

Propose a project inspired by connectome-based predictive modeling and elastic-net regression. These methods can predict cognitive traits from functional connectivity but often fail under dataset shift. Target a more robust metric for out-of-sample prediction across age, scanner, and culture.

Propose a study extending retinal ganglion cell encoding models and convolutional neural networks. CNNs predict retinal responses to simple stimuli but often fail under natural scenes with eye movements and adaptation. Target a better metric for robustness of retinal prosthesis encoding strategies.

Propose a project using Bayesian optimization and closed-loop behavioral training. Current animal training protocols are labor-intensive and depend on hand-tuned reward schedules. Target cheaper compute and faster convergence for individualized task shaping in rodents.

Propose a study building on sleep spindle detection and slow-oscillation closed-loop stimulation. Current algorithms often use fixed thresholds and do not adapt across age or sleep stage. Target a better personalized metric for enhancing memory consolidation during sleep.

Propose a research project extending tract-tracing and viral transsynaptic labeling methods. These approaches reveal circuit connectivity but can suffer from toxicity, incomplete labeling, and uncertain synapse specificity. Target a better validation metric for causal circuit diagrams in the basal ganglia.

Propose a study inspired by variational autoencoders and contrastive learning for neural data compression. Existing compression models reduce dimensionality but may discard rare pathological events or behaviorally relevant transients. Target cheaper storage and a better rare-event preservation metric for long-term neural recordings.

Propose a project using simultaneous EEG-fMRI and joint ICA. These methods combine temporal and spatial resolution but are vulnerable to scanner artifacts and complex cross-modal alignment. Target a better metric for identifying seizure onset zones in epilepsy.

Propose a study extending mean-field models and The Virtual Brain platform. Whole-brain simulations can reproduce resting-state patterns but often lack subject-specific constraints from neurochemistry and microstructure. Target a new domain: personalized simulation of treatment response in major depression.

Propose a research plan building on brain-computer interfaces using Kalman filters and recurrent neural networks. Current decoders can control cursors or robotic arms but require frequent recalibration as neural signals drift. Target a better metric for calibration-free stability in long-term implanted BCIs.

Propose a cognitive model of mind-wandering during reading that builds on the Drift Diffusion Model and ACT-R. Existing models usually capture either response latency or symbolic task control, but not moment-to-moment lapses in comprehension. Target a better trial-level metric predicting both probe-caught mind-wandering reports and subsequent question accuracy.

Propose a low-cost experiment for studying infant statistical learning using the Head-Turn Preference Procedure and artificial grammar learning. These approaches often require specialized labs and exclude remote or low-resource settings. Target a new remote-testing domain with cheaper compute and consumer-grade audio/video equipment.

Propose a model of human category learning that combines the Generalized Context Model and Bayesian Program Learning. The gap is that exemplar models handle graded similarity well, while program-based models explain compositional generalization but are compute-intensive. Target better few-shot generalization to novel categories with reduced inference cost.

Propose a study of cognitive control in bilingual speakers using the Stroop task and the AX-CPT paradigm. Prior work often treats bilingual advantage as a single effect, but task-switching and proactive-control demands are rarely separated. Target a more diagnostic metric that distinguishes proactive from reactive control across language contexts.

Propose a computational account of confirmation bias that integrates Bayesian updating and reinforcement learning. Standard Bayesian accounts explain belief revision under uncertainty, but they often fail to capture motivated avoidance of disconfirming evidence. Target a model that predicts both information-sampling choices and final belief polarization.

Propose a memory intervention based on spaced repetition and retrieval practice for older adults with mild cognitive impairment. These methods improve retention in healthy learners, but their schedules are not optimized for fluctuating attention and fatigue. Target a personalized scheduling metric that improves delayed recall while reducing training time.

Propose a model of visual search in cluttered displays using Feature Integration Theory and Guided Search. These approaches capture bottom-up salience and feature binding, but they struggle with real-world scenes containing semantic context. Target a new domain of naturalistic object search with improved prediction of fixation sequences.

Propose a research design for measuring metacognitive accuracy in reasoning tasks using signal detection theory and confidence calibration curves. Existing studies often confound confidence with response bias and task difficulty. Target a better metric that separates sensitivity, bias, and overconfidence across deductive and probabilistic reasoning.

Propose a cognitive architecture for tool use that extends ACT-R and hierarchical reinforcement learning. Current models can plan symbolic actions or learn motor policies, but they rarely explain flexible transfer to novel tools. Target a new domain of everyday tool improvisation with interpretable subgoal representations.

Propose an experiment on emotional memory using the subsequent memory paradigm and appraisal theory. Prior work shows emotional stimuli are remembered better, but it often collapses valence, arousal, and personal relevance. Target a better metric for predicting item-level recall from separable emotional appraisal dimensions.

Propose a study of numerical cognition in adults with math anxiety using the Approximate Number System framework and Drift Diffusion Model analysis. Existing work documents slower and less accurate responses, but it rarely distinguishes noisy magnitude representation from decision caution. Target a computational metric that separates perceptual precision from response threshold.

Propose a model of causal learning in children that combines causal Bayes nets and the Rescorla-Wagner model. Bayes nets capture abstract causal structure, while associative models capture gradual cue learning, but neither alone explains developmental transitions. Target a better developmental account of when children infer hidden causes versus simple associations.

Propose a language comprehension study using surprisal theory and the N400 event-related potential. Surprisal models predict processing difficulty from word probability, but they often underrepresent discourse-level expectations and pragmatic context. Target improved prediction of comprehension difficulty in multi-sentence narratives.

Propose a social cognition experiment on trust updating using game theory and reinforcement learning. Standard trust games measure cooperative choices, but they often obscure whether people learn partner reliability or infer moral character. Target a model that separately estimates reward learning and trait inference from repeated interactions.

Propose a cognitive model of habit formation that integrates model-free reinforcement learning and implementation intentions. Reinforcement learning explains repetition-based automaticity, but it does not capture explicit goal cues that people use to initiate behavior. Target a practical intervention metric predicting real-world adherence with fewer daily prompts.

Propose a study of auditory scene analysis using the cocktail party paradigm and predictive coding. Classic attention studies show selective listening, but they often use simplified speech streams and ignore active prediction. Target a new domain of noisy multi-speaker conversation with better prediction of comprehension failures.

Propose a computational account of analogy-making that compares Structure-Mapping Theory and Bayesian Program Induction. Structure-Mapping Theory explains relational alignment, but it lacks a precise account of uncertainty and hypothesis search. Target improved performance on novel analogy problems while keeping the model interpretable.

Propose a study of embodied cognition in spatial language using mental rotation tasks and perceptual simulation theory. Prior experiments show motor involvement in language, but they rarely test whether simulation is necessary or merely correlated. Target a causal metric using response interference from congruent versus incongruent body postures.

Propose a model of curiosity-driven exploration using information gap theory and Bayesian active learning. These approaches explain why people seek uncertainty, but they do not distinguish curiosity from reward-seeking in many tasks. Target a behavioral metric that predicts exploration choices when information and reward are experimentally dissociated.

Propose a research program on creativity in problem solving using Remote Associates Test methods and dual-process theory. Existing creativity tasks often provide a single score and cannot distinguish associative search from executive evaluation. Target a better process metric using response trajectories and confidence ratings.

Propose a developmental study of theory of mind using false-belief tasks and violation-of-expectation methods. These approaches often produce different age estimates for mental-state understanding, creating a gap between implicit and explicit measures. Target a unified metric that predicts both looking behavior and verbal explanation.

Propose a model of attention in digital multitasking using the task-switching paradigm and resource theory. Prior work measures switch costs in controlled lab tasks, but it rarely captures self-initiated switching among real apps. Target a new ecological domain with passive logging and a metric for cognitive cost per switch.

Propose an account of implicit bias change using evaluative conditioning and the Implicit Association Test. Current interventions often reduce test scores briefly but fail to produce durable behavior change. Target a longer-term metric linking association strength, explicit goals, and observed social decisions.

Propose a cognitive neuroscience study of working memory using the n-back task and multivariate pattern analysis. The n-back task is widely used, but it confounds storage, updating, and interference control. Target a neural-behavioral metric that isolates maintenance fidelity from executive updating demands.

Propose a model of perceptual decision-making under time pressure using the Drift Diffusion Model and urgency-gating models. Drift diffusion captures evidence accumulation, while urgency-gating captures collapsing decision criteria, but their predictions overlap in standard tasks. Target a diagnostic experimental design that distinguishes accumulation noise from urgency signals.

Propose a study of cultural variation in moral judgment using moral foundations theory and dual-process theory. Existing work often compares countries with translated vignettes but overlooks local norms and interpretation differences. Target a new cross-cultural metric that separates universal harm sensitivity from culturally specific norm endorsement.

Propose a computational model of semantic memory retrieval using Latent Semantic Analysis and spreading activation. LSA captures global semantic similarity, while spreading activation captures associative dynamics, but neither fully predicts retrieval order in verbal fluency tasks. Target improved prediction of cluster switches and response latency in category fluency.

Propose a study of sleep and memory consolidation using targeted memory reactivation and paired-associate learning. TMR can boost recall, but effects vary widely and the mechanisms behind cue timing are unclear. Target a cheaper protocol using wearable sleep staging to optimize cue delivery and improve next-day recall.

Propose a model of human planning in complex games using Monte Carlo Tree Search and model-based reinforcement learning. These methods can plan effectively, but they often require far more simulations than humans appear to use. Target cheaper compute by modeling heuristic pruning and limited-depth search.

Propose a study of language production errors using Dell's spreading activation model and interactive alignment theory. Existing models explain slips of the tongue or dialogue adaptation separately, but not how conversation context changes error patterns. Target a new metric predicting phonological and semantic errors during interactive speech.

Propose a cognitive account of placebo effects using predictive processing and classical conditioning. Conditioning explains learned symptom relief, while predictive processing explains expectation effects, but their relative contributions are hard to separate. Target an experimental design that estimates expectation precision and learned association strength independently.

Propose a study of navigation in aging using cognitive map theory and reinforcement learning. Older adults often shift from allocentric to route-based strategies, but current tasks rarely quantify strategy transitions over time. Target a better metric for identifying when participants switch from map-like planning to stimulus-response navigation.

Propose a model of emotion regulation choice using process model theory and reinforcement learning. The process model describes strategies such as reappraisal and suppression, but it does not explain how people learn which strategy to deploy. Target a computational metric predicting strategy choice from expected effort and emotional payoff.

Propose a study of gesture and learning using embodied cognition and cognitive load theory. Gestures can support explanation and memory, but prior work often ignores when gesture competes with limited working memory. Target a classroom-relevant metric that predicts learning gains from gesture type and task complexity.

Propose a model of face recognition across age and ethnicity using holistic processing theory and signal detection theory. Existing work shows own-age and own-race biases, but it often conflates perceptual sensitivity with response criterion. Target a fairer recognition metric that separates encoding quality from decision bias.

Propose a study of reasoning with misinformation using the continued influence effect and mental model theory. Corrections reduce false belief but often leave causal explanations intact, so people continue relying on misinformation. Target an intervention metric measuring whether participants replace the false causal model, not just reject the false statement.

Propose a computational account of reading difficulties in dyslexia using the dual-route cascaded model and phonological deficit theory. These approaches explain word recognition and phonological decoding, but they often underfit individual differences in compensation strategies. Target personalized prediction of which children benefit most from phonics versus fluency training.

Propose a study of aesthetic preference using processing fluency theory and predictive coding. Prior work links fluency to liking, but it does not explain why moderate novelty can be more pleasing than maximum predictability. Target a metric that predicts aesthetic ratings from the balance of prediction error and perceptual fluency.

Propose a model of social learning in groups using conformity models and Bayesian social inference. Existing conformity models capture majority influence, but they often ignore whether observers infer competence, trustworthiness, or shared goals. Target a better metric predicting when people copy a majority versus a high-competence minority.

Propose a cognitive intervention for reducing smartphone distraction using implementation intentions and attentional control training. These approaches can reduce impulsive checking, but effects often fade because cues are not adapted to individual habits. Target a cheaper, personalized intervention that predicts and blocks high-risk checking episodes with minimal user burden.

Propose a model for daily equity volatility forecasting that builds on GARCH and HAR-RV. These methods capture persistence but underreact to jump risk and news-driven regime shifts. Target a 10% lower out-of-sample QLIKE loss on S&P 500 constituents with no more than twice the compute of HAR-RV.

Design a credit default prediction approach for small-business loans using logistic regression and XGBoost as baselines. Existing scorecards are interpretable but miss nonlinear cash-flow deterioration, while XGBoost often lacks stable explanations for regulators. Target improved AUROC and calibrated probability of default with monotonic feature constraints.

Propose a macroeconomic nowcasting framework that extends Dynamic Factor Models and MIDAS regression. These approaches use mixed-frequency indicators but struggle when high-frequency card spending and mobility data are noisy or revised. Target lower real-time GDP nowcast RMSE during recession-onset quarters.

Develop a portfolio allocation method that improves on mean-variance optimization and Black-Litterman. Both approaches are sensitive to expected-return estimation error and can produce unstable weights in small samples. Target higher out-of-sample Sharpe ratio with turnover below 50% annually.

Propose a bond yield curve forecasting model using Nelson-Siegel and Diebold-Li as reference methods. These models summarize level, slope, and curvature but often fail around monetary policy surprises. Target better 1-month-ahead Treasury yield RMSE at the 2-year and 10-year maturities.

Design an inflation forecasting approach that compares against Phillips Curve models and Bayesian VARs. Traditional models often miss supply-shock episodes and nonlinear pass-through from energy prices. Target improved 6-month-ahead CPI forecast accuracy and calibrated forecast intervals.

Propose a causal study of minimum wage effects using Difference-in-Differences and Synthetic Control. Standard DID can be biased under staggered adoption and heterogeneous treatment effects, while Synthetic Control struggles with many treated units. Target unbiased employment effect estimates for county-level restaurant employment with transparent sensitivity checks.

Develop a household default early-warning model based on survival analysis and random forests. Cox proportional hazards models assume proportional effects, while random forests may ignore time-to-event censoring. Target better concordance index and earlier warning lead time for mortgage delinquency.

Propose a market microstructure model for intraday liquidity using Kyle's lambda and Hasbrouck information share. These measures summarize price impact but do not fully capture liquidity fragmentation across venues. Target better prediction of next-interval bid-ask spread widening for large-cap equities.

Design a financial fraud detection system using isolation forests and graph neural networks. Isolation forests miss coordinated fraud rings, while graph models can be costly and opaque. Target higher precision at 1% recall cost with inference feasible on daily transaction batches.

Propose an exchange-rate forecasting model using uncovered interest parity and LSTM networks as baselines. UIP performs poorly empirically, and LSTMs can overfit short currency histories. Target lower directional error for G10 monthly exchange-rate movements with interpretable macro drivers.

Develop a stress-testing method for banks that builds on CCAR-style scenario analysis and quantile regression. Current scenario templates can miss nonlinear tail interactions between unemployment, rates, and housing prices. Target more accurate 99th percentile loss estimates for consumer loan portfolios.

Propose a sovereign default risk model using Merton-style structural models and probit regression. Structural models simplify fiscal dynamics, while probit models often ignore contagion from regional peers. Target improved calibration of 1-year sovereign spread widening probabilities in emerging markets.

Design a retirement portfolio decumulation strategy comparing against the 4% rule and dynamic programming. The 4% rule is simple but ignores time-varying returns and health spending shocks, while dynamic programming can be computationally expensive. Target higher probability of sustainable consumption with cheaper compute via approximate policy rules.

Propose an ESG factor investing study using Fama-French five-factor models and double machine learning. Existing ESG return studies suffer from omitted variable bias and inconsistent ESG ratings. Target cleaner estimates of ESG alpha across U.S. equities while controlling for rating disagreement.

Develop a consumer spending forecast model using ARIMA and Prophet as benchmarks. These methods handle seasonality but struggle with sudden policy changes such as stimulus payments or tax holidays. Target lower weekly spending forecast MAPE for retail categories during policy-disrupted periods.

Propose an option pricing model that extends Black-Scholes and Heston stochastic volatility. Black-Scholes assumes constant volatility, while Heston can still underprice short-maturity crash risk. Target lower implied-volatility surface RMSE for equity index options with maturities under 30 days.

Design a commodity price forecasting approach using VAR and random forest regression. VAR captures linear cross-commodity relationships but misses nonlinear inventory and weather effects, while random forests can ignore temporal structure. Target improved 3-month-ahead forecast accuracy for crude oil, natural gas, and wheat.

Propose a labor market mismatch index using Beveridge Curve analysis and principal component analysis. These approaches summarize aggregate slack but obscure sectoral skill mismatch and regional frictions. Target an index that predicts wage growth better than the unemployment gap.

Develop a tax compliance risk model using logistic regression and gradient boosting. Logistic models are transparent but miss nonlinear filing anomalies, while boosting can create fairness concerns across income groups. Target higher audit yield with explicit constraints on false positive disparities.

Propose a central bank communication impact model using event studies and BERT-based sentiment analysis. Event studies identify narrow-window market moves but do not quantify the semantic content of statements, while generic BERT may miss monetary-policy language. Target better prediction of intraday yield changes after policy announcements.

Design a housing price model using hedonic regression and spatial econometrics. Hedonic models control for property characteristics but often understate neighborhood spillovers, while spatial lag models may be rigid. Target lower out-of-sample price error for zip-code-level repeat sales.

Propose a firm bankruptcy prediction model using Altman Z-score and neural networks as baselines. Altman Z-score is interpretable but dated for intangible-heavy firms, while neural networks can be hard to audit. Target improved F1 score for public technology firms with feature-level explanations.

Develop a trade flow forecasting model using gravity models and Poisson pseudo-maximum likelihood. Gravity models handle bilateral frictions but often miss supply-chain bottlenecks and geopolitical shocks. Target lower forecast error for quarterly sector-level imports after tariff announcements.

Propose a fiscal multiplier estimation strategy using local projections and structural VARs. SVARs rely on strong identification assumptions, while local projections can be noisy at longer horizons. Target tighter confidence intervals for state-level spending multipliers after infrastructure grants.

Design a risk parity portfolio extension using equal risk contribution and hierarchical risk parity. Equal risk contribution is sensitive to covariance estimation, while hierarchical risk parity may ignore expected returns. Target lower maximum drawdown without reducing annualized return in multi-asset portfolios.

Propose a cryptocurrency liquidity risk model using Amihud illiquidity and GARCH. These methods capture price impact and volatility but miss exchange outages and stablecoin depegging risk. Target better prediction of 1-day liquidation slippage for BTC and ETH perpetual futures.

Develop an insurance claims inflation model using generalized linear models and gradient boosting machines. GLMs are interpretable but miss nonlinear repair-cost dynamics, while GBMs may extrapolate poorly under new inflation regimes. Target improved reserve adequacy for auto insurance claims over 12-month horizons.

Propose a remittance flow forecasting model using ARDL and Bayesian VAR. Existing macro models capture exchange-rate and income channels but underuse migration-network persistence. Target lower quarterly remittance forecast RMSE for low- and middle-income recipient countries.

Design a corporate bond liquidity scoring model using TRACE-based bid-ask measures and PCA. Existing liquidity proxies are sparse for infrequently traded bonds and can be stale. Target a daily liquidity score that predicts transaction cost better than last observed spread.

Propose a price discrimination study for digital platforms using structural demand estimation and causal forests. Structural models can impose restrictive utility forms, while causal forests may lack economic interpretation. Target estimates of heterogeneous consumer surplus effects from personalized discounts.

Develop a bank run early-warning indicator using Diamond-Dybvig theory and panel logit models. Theory clarifies coordination risk but is hard to operationalize, while panel logit models often omit social-media-driven depositor sentiment. Target earlier detection of deposit outflow risk with calibrated false alarm rates.

Propose a supply-chain finance risk model using Altman Z-score and network centrality metrics. Firm-level scores miss contagion through buyer-supplier dependencies, while simple centrality does not distinguish financial fragility. Target better prediction of supplier payment delays in manufacturing networks.

Design a monetary policy rule evaluation using Taylor Rule regressions and reinforcement learning. Taylor Rules are transparent but may be too rigid near the zero lower bound, while reinforcement learning can generate unstable policies. Target lower simulated inflation-output loss with constraints that preserve policy interpretability.

Propose a municipal bond default model using logistic regression and random survival forests. Logistic regression ignores timing of fiscal distress, while survival forests can struggle with rare defaults. Target improved time-dependent AUC for 3-year municipal default risk.

Develop a financial inclusion impact evaluation using randomized encouragement designs and instrumental variables. IV estimates can be local and hard to generalize, while encouragement designs may suffer from weak take-up. Target credible estimates of mobile money access effects on household consumption volatility.

Propose a merger arbitrage spread prediction model using event-study abnormal returns and XGBoost. Event studies capture announcement effects but not evolving regulatory risk, while XGBoost may overfit small deal samples. Target improved probability forecasts of deal completion and lower trading drawdowns.

Design a carbon price pass-through model using input-output analysis and difference-in-differences. Input-output models assume fixed production relationships, while DID may miss heterogeneous firm exposure to energy costs. Target more accurate estimates of consumer price pass-through by industry after carbon tax changes.

Propose a payment network systemic risk model using DebtRank and Eisenberg-Noe clearing networks. Existing network models capture contagion but often assume static exposures and complete information. Target faster intraday identification of critical nodes using streaming payment data.

Develop a cheap-compute macro forecasting benchmark using ridge regression and Bayesian VAR. Bayesian VARs are accurate but can be computationally heavy for many indicators, while ridge regression may miss regime changes. Target comparable forecast RMSE for unemployment and inflation at less than 25% of the compute cost.

Propose a method for forecasting urban heat-island risk by combining graph neural networks with the Weather Research and Forecasting model. Current WRF simulations are physically rich but too expensive for block-level planning, while standard GNNs often ignore energy-balance constraints. Target a 10x reduction in compute while maintaining temperature RMSE within 1 degree Celsius for neighborhood-scale forecasts.

Design an approach for low-cost crop disease diagnosis using CLIP and few-shot prototypical networks. CLIP transfers visual concepts well but is weak on subtle leaf lesions, and prototypical networks degrade under lighting and camera shifts common in smallholder farms. Target improved macro-F1 on unseen regions using fewer than 20 labeled images per disease.

Propose a hybrid model for predicting hospital readmission that combines Cox proportional hazards models with Transformer-based clinical language models. Cox models are interpretable but miss nonlinear text signals, while Transformers can overfit and obscure clinical rationale. Target higher time-dependent AUC with calibrated, clinician-readable risk explanations.

Develop a framework for optimizing public transit schedules using multi-agent reinforcement learning and mixed-integer linear programming. MILP provides feasible schedules but scales poorly under stochastic demand, while MARL can learn adaptive policies but often violates operational constraints. Target reduced passenger waiting time without increasing fleet size or constraint violations.

Propose a method for detecting misinformation cascades by integrating Hawkes processes with graph attention networks. Hawkes processes capture temporal self-excitation but simplify content, while graph attention networks model network structure but often ignore event timing. Target earlier detection at fixed false-positive rate within the first 30 minutes of a cascade.

Design a privacy-preserving system for wearable-based mental health monitoring using federated learning and differential privacy. Federated Averaging reduces raw data sharing but still leaks patterns through updates, while strong differential privacy can destroy weak behavioral signals. Target clinically useful depression-risk AUROC above 0.80 with formal privacy guarantees.

Propose an energy-efficient underwater acoustic communication protocol using orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing and deep reinforcement learning. OFDM handles multipath channels but consumes high power, while DRL policies can adapt but may be unstable under sparse feedback. Target lower bit error rate per joule for autonomous ocean sensors.

Develop a method for reconstructing historical climate extremes by combining data assimilation with Gaussian process regression. Data assimilation uses physical constraints but is limited by sparse archival observations, while Gaussian processes quantify uncertainty but scale poorly in space-time. Target narrower calibrated uncertainty intervals for regional drought reconstructions.

Propose a model for predicting material fatigue in offshore wind turbines using finite element analysis and physics-informed neural networks. FEA is accurate but costly for repeated load scenarios, while PINNs can be faster but struggle with boundary-condition complexity. Target comparable fatigue-life error with at least 20x faster inference.

Design an interdisciplinary framework for personalized nutrition recommendations using causal forests and microbiome variational autoencoders. Causal forests estimate heterogeneous treatment effects but handle high-dimensional microbiome data poorly, while VAEs compress microbiome profiles without causal guarantees. Target better post-meal glucose prediction and interpretable diet-response subgroups.

Propose a method for mapping informal settlements from satellite imagery using U-Net and self-supervised contrastive learning. U-Net needs dense labels that are scarce and inconsistent across cities, while contrastive pretraining may learn features unrelated to settlement morphology. Target higher intersection-over-union in a new city with less than 5 percent labeled training data.

Develop a policy simulation tool for carbon pricing that combines agent-based modeling with computable general equilibrium models. CGE models capture economy-wide effects but assume representative agents, while ABMs capture heterogeneity but often lack macroeconomic consistency. Target more accurate distributional impact estimates across income groups under multiple tax scenarios.

Propose a robotic rehabilitation controller using model predictive control and imitation learning. MPC enforces safety constraints but requires accurate patient-specific dynamics, while imitation learning captures therapist behavior but may fail outside demonstrations. Target improved range-of-motion gains with bounded interaction forces during stroke rehabilitation.

Design a method for real-time flood mapping using Sentinel-1 radar imagery and Bayesian neural networks. Existing thresholding methods are fast but brittle in urban areas, while Bayesian neural networks provide uncertainty but are computationally heavy. Target calibrated flood masks within 10 minutes of image availability on low-cost cloud hardware.

Propose a cross-lingual legal document classifier using XLM-R and label propagation. XLM-R transfers across languages but underperforms on low-resource legal terminology, while label propagation exploits citation networks but can spread noisy labels. Target improved macro-F1 for minority-language court decisions with minimal manual annotation.

Develop a technique for predicting antibiotic resistance by combining genome-wide association studies with graph convolutional networks over protein-interaction networks. GWAS identifies variants but misses pathway-level interactions, while GCNs model biological networks but may over-smooth rare resistance signals. Target higher precision-recall AUC for rare resistance phenotypes.

Propose a system for detecting microplastics in water samples using Raman spectroscopy and random forests. Random forests are robust on tabular spectra but struggle with overlapping polymer signatures, while conventional spectral matching is sensitive to noise and additives. Target higher classification accuracy for mixed-polymer samples using cheaper portable spectrometers.

Design an approach for explainable credit scoring that combines gradient boosting machines with counterfactual explanations. Gradient boosting models achieve strong predictive performance but can encode historical bias, while counterfactual explanations may suggest infeasible financial actions. Target equalized odds improvement while preserving approval-risk calibration.

Propose a multimodal early-warning model for wildfires using ConvLSTM and Bayesian data fusion. ConvLSTM captures spatiotemporal fire dynamics but can ignore sensor reliability, while Bayesian fusion handles uncertainty but may be too simple for nonlinear weather-vegetation interactions. Target earlier detection with fewer false alarms in regions with sparse ground sensors.

Develop a method for optimizing vaccine cold-chain logistics using vehicle routing with time windows and robust optimization. Classical VRPTW methods minimize travel cost but assume stable demand and road conditions, while robust optimization can be overly conservative. Target reduced spoilage and delivery delay under fuel, traffic, and temperature uncertainty.

Propose a framework for studying music therapy effects on anxiety using dynamic causal modeling and recurrent neural networks. Dynamic causal modeling provides interpretable brain-connectivity hypotheses but scales poorly to long sessions, while RNNs capture temporal affect patterns without mechanistic interpretation. Target improved prediction of anxiety reduction from EEG and audio features.

Design a low-resource speech recognition system for endangered languages using wav2vec 2.0 and active learning. wav2vec 2.0 reduces labeled data needs but still requires domain-matched audio, while active learning may oversample noisy or unrepresentative utterances. Target lower word error rate with fewer than 10 hours of transcribed speech.

Propose a method for estimating biodiversity from environmental DNA using DNA metabarcoding and hierarchical Bayesian models. DNA metabarcoding detects cryptic species but produces biased read counts, while hierarchical Bayesian models quantify uncertainty but can be slow for large taxonomies. Target better species richness estimates with calibrated credible intervals at reduced compute cost.

Develop a decision-support model for disaster shelter placement using p-median optimization and participatory GIS. p-median models minimize distance but ignore social vulnerability, while participatory GIS captures local knowledge but is difficult to formalize. Target improved equitable access for elderly and low-income residents without increasing total shelter count.

Propose an approach for automated chemical reaction planning that combines Monte Carlo tree search with graph neural network retrosynthesis. MCTS explores synthesis routes but becomes expensive in large reaction spaces, while GNN retrosynthesis can suggest invalid or unavailable precursors. Target higher valid-route success rate with fewer search expansions.

Design a model for predicting student dropout in online courses using knowledge tracing and survival analysis. Bayesian Knowledge Tracing captures mastery but not time-to-dropout, while survival analysis handles censoring but often ignores learning-state dynamics. Target earlier dropout alerts with improved concordance index and actionable intervention categories.

Propose a smart-grid demand response strategy using deep Q-networks and load flow analysis. Deep Q-networks can learn pricing policies but may violate voltage constraints, while load flow analysis ensures feasibility but is not adaptive to consumer behavior. Target peak-load reduction with zero voltage-limit violations on distribution feeders.

Develop a framework for detecting financial fraud using isolation forests and heterogeneous graph neural networks. Isolation forests detect anomalous transactions but ignore relationships among accounts, merchants, and devices, while graph neural networks can be vulnerable to adversarial edge manipulation. Target higher recall at low false-positive rates under evolving fraud patterns.

Propose a method for language-guided protein design using AlphaFold-style structure prediction and diffusion models. Structure predictors evaluate folded candidates but do not directly generate sequences with desired functional constraints, while diffusion models can generate diverse proteins but may ignore biochemical feasibility. Target higher experimentally plausible sequence diversity for a specified binding motif.

Design a tool for assessing pedestrian safety using computer vision object detection and system dynamics modeling. YOLO-style detectors identify vehicles and pedestrians but provide limited causal insight, while system dynamics models capture feedback loops but rely on coarse manual inputs. Target better prediction of near-miss hotspots from low-cost street cameras.

Propose an algorithm for low-carbon building control using physics-based thermal models and soft actor-critic reinforcement learning. Thermal models are interpretable but require calibration, while soft actor-critic can optimize comfort-energy tradeoffs but needs many unsafe exploration episodes. Target reduced HVAC energy use with no increase in comfort violations during deployment.

Develop a multimodal model for archaeological site discovery using random forest landform modeling and Transformer-based remote-sensing encoders. Random forest models rely on hand-crafted terrain features, while Transformers can exploit imagery but require many labeled sites and may learn survey bias. Target improved recall for undiscovered sites in under-surveyed regions.

Propose a healthcare triage chatbot evaluation method using retrieval-augmented generation and conformal prediction. RAG improves factual grounding but can retrieve outdated guidelines, while conformal prediction provides uncertainty sets but is rarely adapted to clinical dialogue risk. Target safer triage recommendations with calibrated abstention on high-risk symptoms.

Design a method for tracking illegal fishing by combining hidden Markov models and graph-based anomaly detection on vessel AIS data. Hidden Markov models infer vessel states but miss coordinated fleet behavior, while graph anomaly methods detect interactions but often ignore movement physics. Target improved detection precision for dark rendezvous events near marine protected areas.

Propose a framework for modeling human-AI teamwork in air traffic control using cognitive architectures such as ACT-R and partially observable Markov decision processes. ACT-R captures human cognitive constraints but is hard to optimize, while POMDPs formalize uncertainty but simplify operator workload. Target reduced conflict-resolution time without increasing cognitive workload.

Develop a method for predicting food insecurity using satellite vegetation indices and causal impact analysis. NDVI-based models detect crop stress but confound climate shocks with market access, while causal impact methods estimate shocks but require strong counterfactual assumptions. Target more accurate district-level food-insecurity alerts three months in advance.

Propose an explainable model for detecting Parkinsonian gait using dynamic time warping and temporal convolutional networks. Dynamic time warping aligns gait cycles but lacks disease-specific representation learning, while temporal convolutional networks classify sensor streams but are difficult to interpret clinically. Target higher sensitivity for early-stage Parkinson's disease with stride-level explanations.

Design an approach for equitable allocation of urban green infrastructure using maximum coverage optimization and spatial microsimulation. Maximum coverage models maximize population served but can neglect heat-vulnerable groups, while spatial microsimulation estimates demographic exposure but may propagate census uncertainty. Target greater reduction in heat exposure inequality under a fixed planting budget.

Propose a computational method for analyzing deliberative democracy forums using latent Dirichlet allocation and argument mining. LDA summarizes topics but misses argumentative quality, while argument mining identifies claims and evidence but struggles with informal civic language. Target better measurement of viewpoint diversity and evidence use across online town halls.

Develop a method for rapid epidemic scenario planning using SEIR compartmental models and neural ordinary differential equations. SEIR models are interpretable but rigid under behavioral change, while neural ODEs learn flexible dynamics but may extrapolate poorly after interventions. Target faster policy scenario evaluation with improved short-term case forecast accuracy.

Propose a new retrieval-augmented generation method building on RAG and Fusion-in-Decoder. These methods often retrieve passages using shallow similarity and then spend large compute on irrelevant context, so target a 30% reduction in retrieved-token budget while maintaining or improving exact-match accuracy on open-domain QA.

Design a graph neural network architecture that extends GraphSAGE and Graph Attention Networks. Both approaches struggle with oversmoothing and neighbor explosion on billion-edge dynamic graphs, so target higher node-classification F1 with bounded memory and sublinear neighborhood sampling.

Propose a compiler optimization pass inspired by LLVM Polly and TVM. Current polyhedral and tensor compilers require hand-tuned schedules for irregular sparse kernels, so target automatic speedups for sparse matrix multiplication on CPUs and GPUs without degrading dense-kernel performance.

Develop a federated learning algorithm building on FedAvg and FedProx. These methods degrade under severe client heterogeneity and intermittent participation, so target improved test accuracy and fewer communication rounds on non-IID mobile-device datasets.

Propose a database indexing scheme that extends B-trees and learned indexes such as the Recursive Model Index. Learned indexes can fail under distribution shifts and inserts, so target lower tail latency for mixed read-write workloads while preserving correctness guarantees.

Design a malware detection system using ideas from MalConv and graph-based call-graph classifiers. Existing models are brittle to packing, obfuscation, and adversarial byte padding, so target higher robustness to transformed binaries while keeping inference cheap enough for endpoint deployment.

Propose a privacy-preserving analytics framework combining differential privacy mechanisms and secure aggregation. Current deployments often trade away utility for strict privacy or require high communication overhead, so target accurate aggregate statistics with formal privacy guarantees and reduced bandwidth.

Develop a neural program repair approach building on SequenceR and CodeT5. These systems can overfit to small patches and produce syntactically valid but semantically wrong fixes, so target higher plausible-and-correct patch rates on multi-line bugs using test feedback and static constraints.

Propose a reinforcement learning method for robotics that combines Soft Actor-Critic and domain randomization. These approaches can require millions of samples and still transfer poorly to real hardware, so target cheaper sim-to-real learning with fewer real-world calibration trials.

Design a congestion-control algorithm inspired by BBR and PCC Vivace. Existing methods can be unfair or unstable when competing with loss-based flows across variable wireless links, so target higher throughput with bounded latency and improved fairness in mixed networks.

Propose an anomaly detection model for cloud logs building on DeepLog and LogBERT. Prior methods rely on stable log templates and degrade when services evolve rapidly, so target earlier incident detection with fewer false positives in continuously deployed microservices.

Develop a neural architecture search strategy extending DARTS and ENAS. These methods can suffer from search instability and produce architectures optimized for proxy tasks rather than deployment constraints, so target better accuracy-latency tradeoffs on edge devices under a fixed search budget.

Propose a robust image classifier building on Vision Transformers and adversarial training. Standard adversarial training is compute-heavy and often hurts clean accuracy, so target improved robustness to common corruptions and adversarial perturbations with less than 1.5x baseline training cost.

Design a distributed transaction protocol that extends two-phase commit and Calvin. Two-phase commit blocks under coordinator failure while deterministic systems can reduce flexibility for ad hoc workloads, so target serializable transactions with lower abort rates and faster recovery in geo-distributed databases.

Propose a code summarization method using GraphCodeBERT and retrieval-based examples. Current models miss long-range data-flow context and hallucinate behavior not present in code, so target more faithful summaries for large functions while limiting context length and inference cost.

Develop a scheduler for serverless computing inspired by Kubernetes HPA and Knative autoscaling. Existing autoscalers react slowly to bursty workloads and waste resources through cold starts, so target lower p95 latency and reduced idle cost for event-driven applications.

Propose a symbolic-neural theorem proving system building on E prover and Lean-style tactic prediction. Pure symbolic provers face combinatorial explosion while neural tactic models lack reliability, so target higher proof success rates with verifiable proof objects and bounded search time.

Design a video understanding model extending SlowFast Networks and TimeSformer. These methods are expensive on long videos and often miss sparse causal events, so target improved temporal localization accuracy with fewer frames processed per video.

Propose a secure enclave runtime building on Intel SGX and Keystone. Existing enclave systems suffer from side-channel leakage and high overhead for I/O-heavy applications, so target stronger side-channel mitigation with lower latency for confidential microservices.

Develop a recommendation algorithm extending Matrix Factorization and Neural Collaborative Filtering. These approaches struggle with cold-start users and popularity bias, so target higher long-tail item discovery and calibrated recommendations without reducing overall ranking quality.

Propose an energy-aware training system inspired by DeepSpeed ZeRO and gradient checkpointing. Large-model training still wastes energy through inefficient memory movement and static parallelism plans, so target lower joules per training token while preserving throughput and convergence.

Design a static analysis tool for JavaScript building on abstract interpretation and TypeScript-style gradual typing. Existing tools either miss dynamic property behaviors or produce many false alarms, so target higher bug-detection precision for real-world Node.js packages with acceptable analysis time.

Propose a multimodal medical diagnosis model building on CLIP and MedCLIP. These contrastive models can align images and text but may ignore uncertainty and rare pathologies, so target better calibrated predictions for low-prevalence conditions using limited labeled clinical data.

Develop a cache replacement policy combining LRU-K and learned cache policies such as LeCaR. Existing learned policies can overfit workload phases and add inference overhead, so target lower miss ratio with constant-time decisions under changing database workloads.

Propose a human-AI debugging assistant extending Delta Debugging and program slicing. Current debuggers isolate failures but rarely explain causal chains in terms developers can act on, so target faster bug localization and clearer explanations for large concurrent codebases.

Design a blockchain consensus protocol inspired by HotStuff and Tendermint. Current Byzantine fault-tolerant protocols can have high communication cost or slow finality under partial synchrony, so target lower message complexity and sub-second finality for permissioned networks.

Propose a speech recognition model building on wav2vec 2.0 and Conformer. These models perform well with abundant compute but degrade for accented speech and noisy far-field audio, so target lower word error rate for underrepresented accents using limited labeled data.

Develop a data-cleaning system extending HoloClean and active learning. Existing systems depend on hand-written constraints or many human labels, so target higher error-correction accuracy with fewer labeling queries on messy tabular enterprise data.

Propose a neural rendering method building on NeRF and 3D Gaussian Splatting. NeRF is slow to train while Gaussian splatting can struggle with dynamic scenes and view-dependent artifacts, so target real-time rendering of deformable objects with compact memory use.

Design a type-system extension inspired by Rust ownership and linear types. Current ownership systems improve memory safety but can be hard to use for asynchronous shared-state programs, so target stronger data-race prevention with fewer programmer annotations.

Propose a test-generation approach for deep learning libraries building on fuzzing and metamorphic testing. Existing fuzzers find crashes but often miss silent numerical inconsistencies across hardware backends, so target higher detection of wrong-result bugs in tensor compilers and GPU kernels.

Develop an edge inference framework extending TensorRT and ONNX Runtime. Current runtimes optimize individual models but underperform when multiple models share a constrained device, so target lower end-to-end latency and energy for concurrent vision and audio pipelines.

Propose a continual learning algorithm building on Elastic Weight Consolidation and replay buffers. These methods either forget old tasks or require storing many examples, so target higher average accuracy across task streams with a strict memory budget and no access to original datasets.

Design a packet classification algorithm inspired by HyperCuts and decision-tree classifiers. Existing approaches consume large memory for high-dimensional rule sets and update slowly, so target faster lookups with incremental rule updates for software-defined networking switches.

Propose a formal verification workflow combining model checking and Dafny-style deductive verification. Full verification is expensive for distributed protocols with implementation details, so target machine-checked safety properties for production code with reduced annotation burden.

Develop a low-resource machine translation method extending mBART and back-translation. These approaches struggle when the target language has little monolingual data and different morphology from high-resource languages, so target improved BLEU and chrF using synthetic data quality filtering.

Propose a scalable graph query engine building on Pregel and GraphX. Existing systems handle iterative analytics but are inefficient for mixed transactional and analytical graph workloads, so target lower query latency for dynamic property graphs without full recomputation.

Design a fairness-aware classification method extending equalized odds post-processing and adversarial debiasing. These methods can reduce measured bias but may harm calibration or minority-group accuracy, so target improved fairness-accuracy tradeoffs with transparent uncertainty estimates.

Propose a memory-safe C interoperability layer inspired by Checked C and Rust FFI. Current approaches either require large rewrites or leave unsafe pointer aliasing unchecked, so target incremental adoption in legacy C libraries with measurable reduction in memory-safety bugs.

Develop a cloud cost optimizer building on bin packing heuristics and reinforcement-learning schedulers. Existing tools reduce average cost but often violate service-level objectives during demand spikes, so target lower compute spending while maintaining p99 latency guarantees for microservice deployments.

Propose a clinical deterioration prediction model for general medical wards that builds on NEWS2 and the Rothman Index. Both scores underuse free-text nursing notes and often alarm too late in patients with subtle trajectory changes. Target a 20% improvement in event-level sensitivity at the same false-alarm rate while remaining interpretable at the bedside.

Propose a sepsis early-warning approach that compares against SOFA and the Epic Sepsis Model. Existing systems have poor calibration after hospital workflow changes and generate many non-actionable alerts. Target better calibration-in-the-large and a lower number of alerts per true sepsis case in emergency department patients.

Propose a low-compute chest X-ray triage model using CheXNet and DenseNet-121 as baselines. These approaches can perform well but require large labeled datasets and may degrade on portable ICU images. Target comparable AUROC with at least 50% fewer labeled images and inference on a standard hospital CPU.

Propose a method for detecting diabetic retinopathy progression using U-Net and DeepLabV3+ as prior segmentation approaches. Current lesion segmentation pipelines miss small microaneurysms and are sensitive to camera type. Target improved lesion-level F1 across multiple fundus camera vendors without manual domain-specific retraining.

Propose a survival model for heart failure readmission building on Cox proportional hazards and DeepSurv. Traditional Cox models miss nonlinear medication-lab interactions, while neural models often lack clinician trust. Target higher time-dependent C-index with calibrated individual risk explanations suitable for discharge planning.

Propose a neonatal sepsis risk tool using Kaiser Early-Onset Sepsis Calculator and logistic regression as reference methods. These methods rely heavily on maternal risk factors and may underperform when local microbiology patterns shift. Target improved sensitivity for culture-positive sepsis while reducing unnecessary empiric antibiotics.

Propose a radiology report summarization system for oncology follow-up that builds on BERTScore-guided summarization and ClinicalBERT. Existing summarizers can omit interval change and measurable lesion status, which are critical for RECIST-style decisions. Target higher factual consistency and fewer clinically significant omissions than extractive baselines.

Propose a model for predicting acute kidney injury in hospitalized adults using KDIGO criteria and XGBoost as baselines. Current EHR models often fail when creatinine measurement frequency varies and may confuse surveillance intensity with true risk. Target better precision-recall performance in low-testing subgroups and transparent handling of missingness.

Propose a cheaper automated echocardiography view classification pipeline based on ResNet-50 and EfficientNet. Standard CNNs can require high GPU throughput and are not optimized for point-of-care ultrasound devices. Target less than 50 ms inference per clip frame sequence on edge hardware while preserving macro-F1.

Propose a triage strategy for suspected pulmonary embolism that builds on Wells score and YEARS algorithm. These rule-based tools reduce imaging but remain less precise in pregnancy and cancer-associated thrombosis. Target fewer CT pulmonary angiograms per confirmed embolism while maintaining a missed-event rate below accepted safety thresholds.

Propose an ICU mortality prediction framework using APACHE II and SAPS II as comparators. These scores are dated, require manual variables, and calibrate poorly in modern ICUs with changing case mix. Target improved calibration and discrimination using only routinely collected first-6-hour EHR data.

Propose a clinical NLP system for extracting medication adverse events from discharge summaries using cTAKES and Med7 as prior approaches. Rule-based extraction misses negation, temporality, and patient-reported symptoms buried in narrative text. Target higher span-level F1 and reliable distinction between past history, active adverse event, and hypothetical concern.

Propose a digital pathology model for prostate cancer grading using Gleason pattern assessment and CLAM multiple-instance learning as baselines. Whole-slide models often depend on expensive slide-level labels and can fail on rare cribriform patterns. Target improved grade-group accuracy with reduced pathologist annotation time.

Propose a remote monitoring algorithm for COPD exacerbation detection using CAT score trends and LSTM time-series models. Current symptom questionnaires are intermittent, while generic LSTMs may overfit to missing wearable data. Target earlier exacerbation detection with fewer false patient alerts using pulse oximetry, activity, and symptom inputs.

Propose an antimicrobial stewardship recommendation engine building on local antibiograms and Bayesian decision analysis. Existing empiric therapy guidance is static and does not personalize risk for resistant organisms or toxicity. Target improved guideline-concordant coverage with reduced broad-spectrum antibiotic days of therapy.

Propose a stroke onset-to-treatment decision support tool using ASPECTS and RAPID perfusion imaging as references. Current imaging workflows are costly and may delay thrombolysis or thrombectomy decisions in resource-limited hospitals. Target faster treatment eligibility classification using non-contrast CT plus basic clinical variables.

Propose a method for detecting atrial fibrillation from wearables using Pan-Tompkins QRS detection and 1D-CNN baselines. Existing algorithms often confuse motion artifact and ectopy with atrial fibrillation in ambulatory users. Target improved positive predictive value during high-activity periods without increasing battery consumption.

Propose a pediatric asthma hospitalization prediction model building on the Pediatric Asthma Risk Score and random forest classifiers. Current models underuse environmental exposure and prior medication-fill patterns and often generalize poorly across clinics. Target improved AUROC in safety-net pediatric populations and actionable risk strata for preventive outreach.

Propose a multimodal breast cancer response prediction model using RECIST and radiomics with LASSO as baselines. RECIST captures size change but misses early functional response, while hand-crafted radiomics can be unstable across scanners. Target improved prediction of pathologic complete response using MRI, pathology, and treatment timeline data.

Propose a clinical trial eligibility matching system using OMOP Common Data Model queries and Criteria2Query as prior methods. Existing systems struggle with temporal constraints, negated exclusions, and ambiguous lab thresholds. Target higher patient-level recall at fixed reviewer workload in oncology trial screening.

Propose a postoperative delirium prediction tool using CAM-ICU screening and gradient boosting as reference approaches. Current risk models frequently ignore sleep disruption, medication burden, and intraoperative hypotension trajectories. Target better early postoperative AUROC and calibrated risk categories for geriatric surgical patients.

Propose a low-resource dermatology diagnostic assistant using ISIC-trained EfficientNet and nearest-neighbor retrieval as baselines. Models trained on curated dermoscopy images underperform on smartphone images from darker skin tones. Target improved balanced accuracy across Fitzpatrick skin types with explicit uncertainty for referral decisions.

Propose an emergency department patient-flow forecasting model using queuing theory and Prophet time-series forecasting. Current operational models capture volume but not acuity mix, boarding, or staffing constraints. Target lower mean absolute error for 4-hour crowding forecasts and actionable recommendations for bed management.

Propose a clinical note de-identification system using Philter and BiLSTM-CRF as prior approaches. Existing systems often miss rare provider names, locations, and longitudinal identifiers while over-redacting clinically meaningful content. Target higher PHI recall with lower clinical concept loss in multi-institution discharge summaries.

Propose a model for predicting chemotherapy-induced neutropenia using MASCC risk index and elastic net regression as baselines. Existing tools are often cancer-specific and miss dynamic lab recovery patterns between cycles. Target improved cycle-level calibration and identification of patients who would benefit from prophylactic G-CSF.

Propose a chronic kidney disease progression model using eGFR slope and Kidney Failure Risk Equation as comparators. Current models perform poorly for patients with sparse labs and do not account for medication changes such as SGLT2 inhibitors. Target better 2-year kidney failure prediction and interpretable risk trajectories.

Propose a point-of-care ultrasound quality assessment model using SonoNet and MobileNetV3 as prior approaches. Existing quality classifiers are trained on expert-acquired clips and fail when novice operators produce off-axis or incomplete views. Target real-time feedback on mobile hardware with improved novice acquisition success rates.

Propose a maternal morbidity prediction system using the Maternal Early Warning Criteria and logistic regression as reference methods. Current alert systems are threshold-based and generate fatigue, especially for hypertensive disorders and hemorrhage risk. Target higher positive predictive value for severe maternal morbidity within 24 hours of delivery.

Propose a method for detecting malnutrition in hospitalized older adults using MUST and GLIM criteria as baselines. Screening tools depend on manual weight history and may miss sarcopenic obesity or inflammatory states. Target improved sensitivity for dietitian-confirmed malnutrition using routine labs, diagnoses, and nursing intake notes.

Propose a personalized insulin titration assistant using the UVA/Padova simulator and model predictive control as prior approaches. Existing closed-loop strategies may be computationally heavy and brittle during illness, steroids, or irregular meals. Target safer glucose time-in-range with reduced hypoglycemia and feasible deployment on consumer devices.

Propose a diagnostic support model for rare diseases using Human Phenotype Ontology matching and Exomiser as baselines. Current tools often fail when EHR phenotypes are incomplete, noisy, or recorded in nonstandard language. Target higher top-10 diagnostic recall using structured labs, notes, and family history summaries.

Propose a hospital-acquired pressure injury prevention model using Braden Scale and random forest prediction as comparators. Existing risk scores are static and do not incorporate repositioning adherence, vasopressor exposure, or mobility changes. Target improved sensitivity at fixed nursing workload and earlier preventive intervention timing.

Propose a clinical decision aid for syncope evaluation using San Francisco Syncope Rule and Canadian Syncope Risk Score. These tools can be conservative and may not personalize cardiac monitoring or admission decisions. Target fewer low-yield admissions while maintaining 30-day serious outcome sensitivity.

Propose a lung nodule malignancy risk model using Brock model and radiomics-based random forests as baselines. Current models rely on manually measured nodule features and may not generalize to incidental nodules from low-dose CT screening. Target improved calibration and reduced radiologist measurement burden.

Propose a palliative care needs identification model using the Surprise Question and Supportive and Palliative Care Indicators Tool as references. Existing triggers are subjective and often miss non-cancer patients with repeated hospitalizations and symptom burden. Target earlier specialist referral with improved precision among heart failure, COPD, and dementia patients.

Propose a vaccine adverse event signal detection framework using disproportionality analysis and Bayesian Confidence Propagation Neural Network methods. Current pharmacovigilance approaches are vulnerable to stimulated reporting and lack individual clinical context. Target earlier validated signal detection with fewer false positives using EHR-confirmed outcomes.

Propose a blood culture contamination reduction model using standard phlebotomy checklist interventions and logistic regression surveillance as baselines. Current monitoring identifies contamination after the fact and rarely predicts which draws are high risk. Target lower contamination rates by flagging high-risk collection circumstances before specimen submission.

Propose a clinical phenotyping algorithm for long COVID using ICD-code cohorts and PheCode mapping as prior methods. Code-based definitions are inconsistent and miss symptom clusters documented only in notes. Target a more specific and reproducible phenotype using diagnoses, labs, medications, and longitudinal symptom narratives.

Propose an opioid overdose risk prediction tool using the Opioid Risk Tool and LASSO regression as comparators. Existing instruments underperform in patients with polysubstance use, mental health comorbidity, and changing prescription patterns. Target improved near-term overdose prediction with transparent factors for clinician-patient safety planning.

Propose a low-cost tuberculosis triage model using WHO symptom screening and CAD4TB chest X-ray analysis as baselines. Symptom screening has low specificity, while commercial CAD systems may be expensive for primary clinics. Target higher specificity than symptom screening with deployable compute requirements for rural health centers.

Compare Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) and TD3 for torque-controlled quadruped locomotion on low-cost motors with backlash and latency. Both methods can learn robust gaits in simulation, but their policies often exploit ideal actuator assumptions and degrade after deployment. Propose a control approach targeting at least 20% lower sim-to-real tracking error while keeping training compute under 2 GPU-days.

Model Predictive Path Integral control (MPPI) and Cross-Entropy Method (CEM) planners handle nonlinear dynamics but require many rollouts per control step. This limits their use on embedded robot arms with 50-100 Hz control loops. Propose a cheaper sampling-based controller that preserves collision avoidance while reducing online compute by at least 3x.

iLQR and Differential Dynamic Programming (DDP) provide efficient trajectory optimization for smooth dynamics, but they struggle with contact-rich manipulation because contact switches create discontinuities. Propose a contact-aware variant for pushing and pivoting objects on a table. Target higher task success than standard iLQR on at least three object shapes without hand-tuned contact schedules.

Behavior Cloning (BC) and DAgger are common for learning robot manipulation policies from demonstrations. BC suffers from compounding errors, while DAgger requires repeated expert intervention that is costly on physical robots.
